title: List of alternative rheology modifiers for use in the production of hand sanitizers - Canada.ca
content:
## List of alternative rheology modifiers for use in the production of hand sanitizers
Many ingredients can be considered as replacements for a rheology modifier used in a clear gel (e.g. a hydroalcoholic gel hand sanitizer). The list below contains examples of replacement rheology modifiers which can be used to form a clear or slightly hazed gel (please verify with each supplier); other ingredients may also be commercially available:
Chemical substance: Carboxypolymethylene
- BASF Rheocare C Plus
Chemical substance: Carbomer 940
Chemical substance: Carbomer 981
Chemical substance: Carbomer 980
Chemical substance: Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer
Chemical substance: Ammonium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/VP Copolymer
Chemical substance: Hypromellose/Cellulose, 2-hydroxypropyl methyl ether/Cellulose hydroxypropyl methyl ether/Carbohydrate gum/Hydroxypropylmethylcellulose/Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose
Chemical substance: Acrylic Acid/VP Crosspolymer
Chemical substance: Silicic acid, lithium magnesium sodium salt/Lithium magnesium sodium silicate/Silicic acid, Lithium magnesium sodium salt
Chemical substance: Hydroxypropylcellulose/Cellulose, 2-hydroxypropyl ether/Hyprolose
Chemical substance: Acrylates Copolymer
Chemical substance: Polyquaternium-37
- BASF Cosmedia Ultragel 300
Chemical substance: Polyquaternium-10
Recent technologies can also be considered:
Chemical substance: Microcrystalline cellulose
If product transparency is not a requirement for a given application, other alternatives could be considered, including:
Chemical substance: Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer
Chemical substance: Polyquaternium-37 and Dicaprylyl Carbonate and Lauryl Glucoside
- BASF Cosmedia Triple C
Chemical substance: Hydroxyethyl Acrylate/Sodium Acryloyldimethyl Taurate Copolymer
Chemical substance: Polyacrylate-13 and Polyisobutene and Polysorbate 20
Chemical substance: Acrylamide ̸Sodium Acryloyldimethyl Taurate Copolymer ̸Isohexadecane ̸Polysorbate 80
Chemical substance: Ammonium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/Beheneth-25 Methacrylate Crosspolymer
- This is not an exhaustive list
- As other suitable alternatives are identified, they will be added to the list
- Companies with existing formulations are encouraged to work with a chemist to properly compare specification sheets and perform preliminary tests prior to purchasing ingredients
- If you are considering natural gums as a replacement rheology modifier, it is important to assess their resistance to the high concentrations of ethanol (or other alcohol)
- Companies are responsible for verifying the accuracy of the formulation and the approval status of an ingredient based on the CAS (Chemical Abstracts Service) number or ingredient INCI (International Nomenclature of Cosmetic Ingredients) name
- Companies are responsible to respect existing Intellectual Property laws. It is recommended to consult with a specialized patent agent
This content was prepared and reviewed jointly by Health Canada and the Natural Research Council of Canada, with collaboration from Dr. Ripoll, Ph.D., Professor, DESS (Specialized Graduate Diploma) in cosmetology at UQAC and Chair of Quebec Chapter, Society of Cosmetic Chemists.
